name: Transformer Architecture
shortDesc: The neural network architecture underlying GPT, BERT, and modern LLMs, utilizing self-attention mechanisms.

steps.label: Tokenization & Embedding
steps.desc: Raw text is split into tokens and converted into dense vectors of fixed dimension.

steps.label: Positional Encoding
steps.desc: Adds positional information to embeddings since the model has no inherent sense of order.

steps.label: Encoder Stack (xN)
steps.desc: A stack of identical layers processing the input sequence in parallel.

children.label: Multi-Head Self-Attention
children.desc: Allows the model to attend to different positions in the sequence to capture contextual relationships.

children.label: Add & Norm
children.desc: Applies residual connections followed by Layer Normalization to stabilize training and gradient flow.

children.label: Feed Forward Network
children.desc: Two linear transformations with a non-linear activation function (e.g., ReLU or GELU).

steps.label: Decoder Stack (xN)
steps.desc: Generates the output sequence one token at a time, attending to previously generated tokens and the encoder output.

children.label: Masked Self-Attention
children.desc: Attention mechanism that prevents the model from seeing future tokens during training.

children.label: Decoder Cross-Attention
children.desc: Attends to the final output of the Encoder stack.

children.label: Output Projection
children.desc: Projects hidden states into the vocabulary space to predict the next token.

name: Neural Network (FFNN)
shortDesc: The foundational architecture for Deep Learning, consisting of an Input, Hidden, and Output layer.

steps.label: Input Layer
steps.desc: Receives the raw data features and passes them to the hidden layer.

steps.label: Hidden Layers
steps.desc: Layers where weights and biases are applied to extract non-linear features.

children.label: Weighted Sum
children.desc: Calculates dot product of inputs and weights plus bias term.

children.label: Activation Function
children.desc: Introduces non-linearity (e.g., ReLU, Sigmoid) to the network.

children.label: Dropout
children.desc: Randomly deactivates neurons during training to prevent overfitting.

steps.label: Output Layer
steps.desc: Produces final probabilities (classification) or continuous values (regression).

name: RAG Architecture
shortDesc: Retrieval-Augmented Generation enhances LLMs by dynamically retrieving external knowledge from a vector database.

steps.label: User Prompt
steps.desc: The user's question or query.

steps.label: Retriever
steps.desc: Encodes the query and searches a Vector Database for relevant documents.

steps.label: Prompt Augmentation
steps.desc: Injects the retrieved documents into the user's prompt to provide context.

steps.label: LLM Generation
steps.desc: The Language Model generates an answer based on the augmented context and query.

name: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)
shortDesc: A specialized architecture for processing grid-like data such as images, using filters and pooling.

steps.label: Input Image
steps.desc: The raw pixel data (Height x Width x Channels).

steps.label: Convolutional Layer
steps.desc: Applies learnable filters (kernels) to the input to extract features like edges and textures.

children.label: Convolution Operation
children.desc: Element-wise multiplication of the kernel with the input patch.

children.label: ReLU Activation
children.desc: Introduces non-linearity to the network.

steps.label: Pooling Layer
steps.desc: Reduces the spatial dimensions (Downsampling) to decrease computation and control overfitting.

steps.label: Flattening
steps.desc: Converts the 3D feature maps into a 1D vector for the fully connected layers.

steps.label: Output Layer
steps.desc: Final fully connected layer to produce class probabilities or values.

name: U-Net Architecture
shortDesc: A symmetrical architecture commonly used for image segmentation, featuring a contracting path and an expanding path.

steps.label: Input Image
steps.desc: The source image to be segmented.

steps.label: Contracting Path (Encoder)
steps.desc: Downsamples the image to extract context features using Conv and Pooling.

children.label: Double Convolution
children.desc: Two unpadded convolutions to preserve spatial information.

children.label: Max Pooling
children.desc: Reduces the height and width, capturing the most important features.

steps.label: Bottleneck
steps.desc: The feature vector passed from the encoder to the decoder.

steps.label: Expanding Path (Decoder)
steps.desc: Upsamples the feature map back to the original input resolution using transposed convolutions.

children.label: Transposed Convolution
children.desc: Upsamples the feature map using learned filters.

children.label: Concatenation
children.desc: Combines upsampled features with the skip connections from the encoder.

children.label: Double Convolution
children.desc: Refines the upsampled features to recover spatial details.

steps.label: Output Mask
steps.desc: 1x1 Conv layer that maps features to class probabilities for each pixel.

name: Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)
shortDesc: A framework where two neural networks compete: a Generator creating fake data and a Discriminator distinguishing real from fake.

steps.label: Random Noise Vector
steps.desc: A random noise vector (e.g., Gaussian) acts as the seed for generation.

steps.label: Generator Network
steps.desc: Takes noise as input and generates a synthetic data sample (e.g., a fake image).

steps.label: Discriminator Network
steps.desc: Takes both real data and fake samples as input and outputs a probability of realness.

children.label: Real Data In
children.desc: Passes a real sample from the training set.

children.label: Fake Data In
children.desc: Passes the generated sample from the Generator.

steps.label: Backpropagation
steps.desc: Gradients are calculated based on the Discriminator's feedback to update the Generator.

name: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)
shortDesc: A Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) designed to process sequences of data by maintaining an internal 'Cell State'.

steps.label: Input Sequence
steps.desc: The sequence of data (e.g., a sentence) fed into the network.

steps.label: Forget Gate
steps.desc: Decides what information to throw away from the cell state.

steps.label: Input Gate
steps.desc: Decides what new information from the current input to store in the cell state.

steps.label: Cell State Update
steps.desc: Calculates the new candidate cell state and hidden state to be carried forward.

steps.label: Output Gate
steps.desc: Decides what part of the cell state to expose to the next layer.

steps.label: Hidden State
steps.desc: The output passed to the next time step.

name: Autoencoder
shortDesc: An architecture trained to copy its input to its output, used for dimensionality reduction and feature learning.

steps.label: Input Data
steps.desc: The raw data (e.g., image or text) passed into the network.

steps.label: Encoder
steps.desc: Compresses the input into a lower-dimensional 'Bottleneck' representation.

children.label: Encoder Conv Layers
children.desc: Layers that extract features and reduce spatial dimensions.

children.label: Bottleneck Layer
children.desc: The compressed latent space vector containing the essential information.

steps.label: Decoder
steps.desc: Reconstructs the input from the bottleneck vector.

children.label: Decoder Conv Layers
children.desc: Layers that upsample the bottleneck back to the original dimensions.

children.label: Output
children.desc: The reconstructed data.

name: Diffusion Model (DDPM)
shortDesc: Generates data (like images) by gradually removing noise from random static.

steps.label: Input Noise
steps.desc: Start with pure random noise (Gaussian distribution).

steps.label: Forward Diffusion Process
steps.desc: Iteratively adds noise to the data over T steps.

children.label: Step t (T to 1)
children.desc: Adds a small amount of Gaussian noise to the data sample.

steps.label: Reverse Diffusion (Inference)
steps.desc: The neural network (U-Net) learns to predict the noise added at each step.

steps.label: Denoising
steps.desc: Predicts the noise to subtract, gradually restoring the structured data.
